description: You are a Precalculus teacher creating an interactive GeoGebra applet that demonstrates how all conic sections (circle, ellipse, parabola, hyperbola) are one unified curve family controlled by a single parameter: eccentricity. Create a construction in GeoGebra featuring an eccentricity slider named 'e' (range 0 to 2, increment 0.01), a fixed semi-latus rectum value l = 2, and a focus point F at the origin (0, 0). Include a parametric conic curve defined by the polar focus-directrix equation r = l / (1 + e * cos(θ)), implemented using the Cartesian parametric form: Curve[ (l/(1 + e*cos(t))) * cos(t), (l/(1 + e*cos(t))) * sin(t), t, 0, 6.28 ]. Add at least one text annotation on the canvas describing the conic type or the eccentricity value. Save the construction as 'conic_eccentricity.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/
